rationale: Both papers center on how brand/product positioning shapes consumer evaluation processes: the citing paper studies functional vs experiential positioning effects on evaluation, while the cited paper develops a positioning-based judgment-revision mechanism (search/alignment, diagnosticity, accessibility). Given this strong construct overlap and the cited paper’s explicit “implications for brand positioning,” it is plausibly a theory/mechanism source rather than an incidental mention. Even though the raw evidence is only a DOI match (no local citation context), it is not safe to drop.
